Things to Do

Go where refined meets rustic, where history meets community, and where new meets old. Situated at the epicenter of Texas’s five major metros, Bryan makes for the ultimate getaway.

Little Boots, Tall Tales

The Legend Of Bryan, Texas

In Bryan, Texas, you'll travel back in time, come face-to-face with wild beasts, meet queens, and even learn to fly – if you believe our friend here, that is.

With something here for everyone — from happy wanderers to outdoor enthusiasts, avid adventurers, history buffs, famished foodies, and family-focused travelers alike — this legendary Texas town is full of surprises you won’t soon forget. Visit Bryan for
all things
history, art, music, shopping, and food.

Texas Triangle”, Bryan, Texas is conveniently located less than three…

Explore a truly unique and vibrant city steeped in more than 150 years of culture.

You’ll find plenty of wide open spaces in the Brazos Valley with so many ways to relax, recreate, and connect with nature at our dozens of parks, world-class golf courses, outdoor events, lakeside trails, and more.

In Historic Downtown Bryan, the legendary stories of our past converge with a thriving arts scene, top-rated restaurants, signature events, and eclectic boutiques & antique shops to form the ever-evolving cultural hub of the Brazos Valley.

With plentiful and varied attractions and accommodations to explore, your visit is guaranteed to be a hit.

Whether it’s a live concert along Lake Walk, a stroll through the Farmer's Market in Brazos County, or a festival in Historic Downtown Bryan, our locals love to gather for food, music, and fun — regardless of the reason or the season!

College Station invites visitors to explore a dynamic mix of history, culture and play. From nationally recognized attractions like the George H.W. Bush Presidential Library and Museum to the iconic traditions found across the Texas A&M University campus, there is always something happening in Aggieland.

Spend the day outdoors in local parks and trails, dive into hands-on entertainment like axe throwing and ice skating or experience the electric atmosphere of game day at Kyle Field. When the sun goes down, enjoy live music, local nightlife and a growing dining scene that keeps visitors coming back for more.

Outdoors

College Station makes it easy to get outside and enjoy wide-open spaces, scenic trails and parks designed for every pace. Whether you are looking to take a leisurely walk, enjoy a picnic, explore local greenways or spend time with family, the city’s outdoor spaces offer a refreshing way to slow down and reconnect.

It is the perfect complement to College Station’s lively energy and welcoming spirit.

Art Galleries & Museums

Art galleries and museums in College Station offer a deeper look at the stories, creativity and innovation that shape the city. From nationally recognized institutions to local galleries and rotating exhibits, visitors can explore everything from history and heritage to contemporary art and culture. It is an engaging way to experience College Station beyond the game day atmosphere and discover what makes Aggieland unique.

Events

Events in College Station bring the community and visitors together all year long. From live music and local markets to sporting events, festivals and family-friendly experiences, there is always something happening in the Heart of Aggieland.

Whether you are planning a weekend visit or looking to experience the city like a local, College Station’s event calendar offers memorable moments that showcase its energy, traditions and unmistakable sense of place. Depending on the time of year, take advantage of vibrant happenings in entertainment, sports, food, the arts and more.

Hosting an event in College Station? Whether it is a festival, concert, market or community gathering, we would love to help spread the word. Local businesses and event organizers are invited to submit their event details to be featured on our calendar and shared with visitors and locals alike.
